info.name: Adblock Compiler API
info.description:
Auto-generated from docs/api/openapi.yaml. Run 'deno task postman:collection' to regenerate.

**Compiler-as-a-Service** for adblock filter lists. Transform, optimize, and combine filter lists from multiple sources with real-time progress tracking.

## Features
- 🎯 Multi-Source Compilation
- ⚡ Performance (Gzip compression, caching, request deduplication)
- 🔄 Circuit Breaker with retry logic
- 📊 Visual Diff between compilations
- 📡 Real-time progress via SSE and WebSocket
- 🎪 Batch Processing
- 🌍 Universal (Deno, Node.js, Cloudflare Workers, browsers)

## Links
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/jaypatrick/adblock-compiler)
- [Documentation](https://github.com/jaypatrick/adblock-compiler/tree/master/docs)
- [Web UI](https://adblock-compiler.jayson-knight.workers.dev/)

item.name: Compilation
item.description: Filter list compilation endpoints

item.name: Compile filter list (JSON)
item.description: Compile filter lists and return results as JSON. Results are cached for 1 hour.

request.description:
Compile filter lists and return results as JSON. Results are cached for 1 hour.
Supports request deduplication for concurrent identical requests.

item.description: Compile multiple filter lists in parallel (max 10 per batch)

request.description: Compile multiple filter lists in parallel (max 10 per batch)

item.name: Parse filter rules into AST
item.description: Parses adblock filter rules into an Abstract Syntax Tree representation

request.description: Parses adblock filter rules into an Abstract Syntax Tree representation

item.name: Streaming
item.description: Real-time event streaming endpoints

item.name: Compile with real-time progress (SSE)
item.description: Compile filter lists with real-time progress updates via Server-Sent Events.

request.description:
Compile filter lists with real-time progress updates via Server-Sent Events.
Streams events including source downloads, transformations, diagnostics, cache operations, network events, and metrics.

item.name: WebSocket
item.description: Bidirectional real-time communication

item.name: WebSocket endpoint for real-time compilation
item.description: Bidirectional WebSocket connection for real-time compilation with event streaming.

request.description:
Bidirectional WebSocket connection for real-time compilation with event streaming.

**Client → Server Messages:**
- `compile` - Start compilation
- `cancel` - Cancel running compilation
- `ping` - Heartbeat ping

**Server → Client Messages:**
- `welcome` - Connection established
- `pong` - Heartbeat response
- `compile:started` - Compilation started
- `event` - Compilation event (source, transformation, progress, diagnostic, cache, network, metric)
- `compile:complete` - Compilation finished successfully
- `compile:error` - Compilation failed
- `compile:cancelled` - Compilation cancelled
- `error` - Error message

**Features:**
- Up to 3 concurrent compilations per connection
- Automatic heartbeat (30s interval)
- Connection timeout (5 minutes idle)
- Session-based compilation tracking
- Cancellation support

item.name: Migrate D1 data to PostgreSQL
item.description: One-time migration that reads data from Cloudflare D1 (SQLite) and writes it to PostgreSQL via Hyperdrive.

query.description: Comma-separated list of tables to migrate (default: all)

request.description:
One-time migration that reads data from Cloudflare D1 (SQLite) and writes it to PostgreSQL via Hyperdrive.

Migrates the following tables: `storage_entries`, `filter_cache`, `compilation_metadata`.

The migration is:
- **Idempotent**: uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING to skip existing rows
- **Batched**: processes rows in chunks of 100
- **Read-only on D1**: never modifies the source database
- **Resumable**: can be re-run safely if interrupted

item.name: Browser Rendering
item.description: Endpoints backed by the Cloudflare Browser Rendering binding (`BROWSER`). Used for resolving redirect chains and monitoring filter-list source URLs for changes.

item.name: Resolve canonical URL via browser
item.description: Navigates to a URL using the Cloudflare Browser Rendering binding and returns the

request.description:
Navigates to a URL using the Cloudflare Browser Rendering binding and returns the
final canonical URL after all redirects. Useful for discovering the true destination
of redirect chains or URL shorteners before scheduling a filter-list download.

Requires the `BROWSER` binding to be configured in `wrangler.toml`.

item.name: Monitor filter-list sources for changes
item.description: Fetches each provided URL using the Cloudflare Browser Rendering binding and

request.description:
Fetches each provided URL using the Cloudflare Browser Rendering binding and
computes a content hash. The result is compared against the previously stored hash
(if any) in `COMPILATION_CACHE` KV. Each source is reported as `changed` or
`unchanged`. The latest result set is persisted under the KV key
`browser:monitor:latest` and is retrievable via `GET /api/browser/monitor/latest`.

Requires both the `BROWSER` and `COMPILATION_CACHE` bindings.

item.name: Retrieve the latest monitor results
item.description: Returns the most recent result set written by `POST /api/browser/monitor`. Reads

request.description:
Returns the most recent result set written by `POST /api/browser/monitor`. Reads
the KV key `browser:monitor:latest` from `COMPILATION_CACHE`.

Requires the `COMPILATION_CACHE` binding.
